# web-nextjs Template

A production-ready Next.js 16 web application template with Supabase Auth, shadcn/ui, and internationalization support.

## What Is It?

`web-nextjs` is a full-stack web application scaffold built on the AgentDock four-layer architecture. It provides:

- **Authentication** — Email/password + GitHub OAuth via Supabase Auth
- **UI Components** — shadcn/ui with dark mode support
- **Internationalization** — next-intl for multi-language apps (en/zh)
- **Form Handling** — react-hook-form + Zod validation
- **Four-Layer Architecture** — Clear separation of concerns (features → core → infra → app)

## Problem It Solves

Building a modern web app from scratch involves many repetitive decisions:

| Pain Point | How web-nextjs Helps |
|---|---|
| Auth setup | Pre-configured Supabase Auth with email/password + OAuth |
| UI consistency | shadcn/ui components with unified design tokens |
| i18n complexity | next-intl with locale-based routing |
| Architecture drift | Four-layer structure enforced by ESLint rules |
| Form validation | react-hook-form + Zod schemas in a shared location |
| AI agent confusion | Clear directory contracts and `AGENTS.md` for Copilot |

## Why This Template?

### For Solo Developers
- Start building features immediately — no boilerplate setup
- Auth, i18n, and UI are pre-integrated
- Layer 2 rules prevent architectural mistakes

### For Teams
- Consistent structure across projects
- Easy onboarding — new devs learn one pattern
- AI agents can navigate the codebase reliably

### For AI Coding Assistants
- `AGENTS.md` defines execution boundaries
- Directory contract tells agents where to put code
- OpenSpec changes provide structured planning

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack) |
| Language | TypeScript 5.9 |
| Auth | Supabase Auth (email/password + GitHub OAuth) |
| UI | shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS 4 |
| Forms | react-hook-form + Zod |
| i18n | next-intl |
| State | React Server Components + Server Actions |
| Icons | @tabler/icons-react, lucide-react |
| Notifications | sonner (toast) |
| Linting | ESLint 9 (flat config) + Prettier |
| Package Manager | pnpm 9+ |

## Architecture

```
src/
├── app/                    # App layer - routes, layouts, pages
│   ├── [locale]/          # Locale-scoped routes
│   │   ├── (auth)/        # Public auth routes (login, signup)
│   │   └── (protected)/   # Authenticated routes (dashboard, settings)
│   └── auth/              # Auth callback handlers
├── components/            # Shared UI components
│   ├── ui/               # shadcn/ui primitives
│   ├── dashboard/         # Dashboard-specific components
│   └── profile/           # Profile form components
├── core/                  # Core layer - types, interfaces, domain logic
│   ├── types/            # TypeScript types (AuthUser, ActionResult, etc.)
│   └── repositories/     # Repository interfaces (IAuthRepository)
├── features/             # Feature layer - use cases, server actions
│   └── auth/             # Auth feature (actions, contract, index)
├── infra/                # Infrastructure layer - external services
│   ├── db/              # Supabase client & repository implementations
│   └── providers.ts     # DI factory (getAuthRepository)
└── lib/                  # Shared utilities
    └── validations/      # Zod schemas
```

### Four-Layer Flow

```
User Request
    ↓
app/ (Route Handler / Page)
    ↓
features/ (Server Action - use case orchestration)
    ↓
core/ (Repository Interface - contract)
    ↓
infra/ (Repository Implementation - Supabase SDK)
```

### Layer 2 Constraint

The template enforces a critical rule via ESLint:

> **Features must NOT import from `@/infra/*` directly.**

This ensures business logic stays independent of infrastructure details. Use repository interfaces from `core/repositories` instead.

## Completed Features

### Authentication
- [x] Email/password sign-in
- [x] Email/password sign-up with verification flow
- [x] GitHub OAuth integration
- [x] Password reset (forgot-password → email → reset-password)
- [x] Session management (getCurrentUser helper)
- [x] Protected route groups (`(protected)` layout)
- [x] Auth middleware (session refresh + locale detection)

### User Account
- [x] Profile page with avatar display
- [x] Display name update
- [x] Email display (read-only)
- [x] Password change entry point (redirects to forgot-password)

### Navigation
- [x] Collapsible sidebar with user menu
- [x] Settings navigation (profile, help, privacy, about)
- [x] Clean nav-user dropdown (Account link, Log out)
- [x] No placeholder `#` links

### Pages
- [x] Login page
- [x] Sign-up page with email verification state
- [x] Dashboard page
- [x] Forgot password page
- [x] Reset password page
- [x] Profile settings page
- [x] Help page (placeholder)
- [x] Privacy policy page (placeholder)
- [x] About page (placeholder)

### Internationalization
- [x] English (en) and Chinese (zh) locales
- [x] Locale-based URL routing (`/[locale]/...`)
- [x] next-intl translation hooks
- [x] Translation files in `messages/`

## Development Workflow

### Local Setup

```bash
# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Set up environment variables
cp .env.example .env.local
# Edit .env.local with your Supabase credentials:
# NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=your-project-url
# NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key
# SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=your-service-role-key
# NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000

# Run development server
pnpm dev
```

### Verification Commands

```bash
# Type check
pnpm check-types

# Lint (includes Layer 2 constraint checks)
pnpm lint

# Build for production
pnpm build

# Format check
pnpm format
```

### Using CLI for Real-Environment Validation

The AgentDock CLI can create a new project from this template and deploy it:

```bash
# Create a new project from the template
npx @cogito.ai/cli create my-app --template web-nextjs

# Navigate to the project
cd my-app

# Install and configure Supabase
# Follow the prompts to set up your Supabase project

# Deploy to Vercel
npx vercel deploy --prod
```

## Configuration

### Supabase Setup

1. Create a new Supabase project at [supabase.com](https://supabase.com)
2. Enable Email provider in Authentication → Providers
3. Enable GitHub OAuth (optional):
   - Register a GitHub OAuth app
   - Add Client ID and Secret to Supabase
4. Configure redirect URLs:
   - `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback`
   - `https://your-domain.com/auth/callback`

### Adding New Locales

1. Add locale to `src/i18n/config.ts`:
   ```ts
   export const locales = ['en', 'zh', 'ja'] as const
   ```
2. Create translation file: `messages/ja.json`
3. Update middleware if needed

### Adding New Features

Follow the four-layer pattern:

1. **Define types** in `src/core/types/`
2. **Define repository interface** in `src/core/repositories/` (if needed)
3. **Implement repository** in `src/infra/db/`
4. **Create Server Actions** in `src/features/<feature>/actions.ts`
5. **Create pages** in `src/app/[locale]/...`
6. **Add translations** in `messages/en.json` and `messages/zh.json`

## Non-Goals (Intentionally Excluded)

These features are deliberately NOT included to keep the template focused:

- Database ORM (Prisma, Drizzle) — use Supabase PostgREST directly or add your own
- Testing framework — add Vitest/Jest as needed
- E2E testing — add Playwright/Cypress as needed
- Analytics — integrate PostHog, Plausible, etc. as needed
- Payment/Billing — add Stripe when required
- Real-time features — add Supabase Realtime when needed
- Admin panel — build separately or add when needed

## Migration Guide

### From Create Next App

1. Copy your `app/` pages into `src/app/[locale]/`
2. Move shared components to `src/components/`
3. Extract business logic into `src/features/`
4. Move types to `src/core/types/`
5. Wrap external services in `src/infra/`

### From Other Templates

1. Review the four-layer architecture
2. Map your existing structure to the layer contract
3. Add repository interfaces for external services
4. Move Server Actions to `src/features/`
5. Update imports to follow layer boundaries

## Troubleshooting

### "Direct DB access in features is not allowed"

You're importing from `@/infra/db/client` in a feature file. Use the repository pattern instead:

```ts
// Wrong - in src/features/auth/actions.ts
import { getServerClient } from '@/infra/db/client'

// Correct
import { getAuthRepository } from '@/infra/providers'
const repo = getAuthRepository()
await repo.someMethod()
```

### "Module not found: Can't resolve '@/xxx'"

Check your `tsconfig.json` paths configuration:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Auth callback returns error

Verify your Supabase redirect URLs include:
- `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback`
- `https://your-domain.com/auth/callback`
